codeReview.summary: The new cross-flow suite points at the right requirements, but it stays at service/export level and uses weak assertions, so the actual Dashboard-vs-Session-Detail launch contract is still not locked down.

issues.description: `assembleAnchored`/`assembleUnanchored` call `ContextPackService.assemble` directly, so none of the VAL-CTXCROSS tests exercise `ContextPackSheet` from `DashboardView` or `SessionDetailView`. A regression in `anchorSessionId`, `anchorProject`, or `dateRange` wiring would still pass, which means the actual cross-entry launch path is unverified.

The parity assertions are too weak to enforce the contract: `test_dashboardAndSessionDetailProduceEquivalentPackForSameAnchor` compares anchored vs unanchored packs and only checks count/presence, `test_sameAnchorSemanticParityAcrossEntrypoints` only checks substring presence, and `test_targetSwitchChangesEnvelopeOnly` only checks envelope markers plus one session-id substring. None of them assert ordered session-id equality or normalized shared-body equality, so same-anchor and envelope-only regressions can slip through.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `context-pack-ui-worker` procedure should explicitly require exercising the actual launch harness (`ContextPackSheet` from Dashboard and Session Detail) for cross-flow work. The current procedure allowed service-level assertions to stand in for entrypoint wiring, which leaves anchor and ambient-scope regressions undetected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackCrossFlowTests.swift:140-157 defines direct service helpers, and the VAL-CTXCROSS tests never instantiate `DashboardView`, `SessionDetailView`, or `ContextPackSheet`.

summary: Fail: the new tests don't actually prove the cross-entry launch contract, so the milestone's same-anchor, precedence, and lifecycle invariants can still regress without breaking this suite.
